intent.objective: Run the canonical Refine loop for <target> and produce RESULT.md.

route_menu.context_hint: Refine always preserves the canonical ten-stage loop, then selects technique overlays that change validation expectations or stage configuration.

items.label: Baseline sequence
items.description: Use the canonical loop with sequence, handoff, validation, owner-boundary, and observability techniques.

items.label: Tension or alternatives
items.description: Add dialectic, tournament, Pareto, and recomposition techniques when competing designs must be compared.

items.label: Structure or test
items.description: Add x-ray, toy-game, memory, protected-context, or standards techniques when the target needs explanation or falsification.

technique_overlays.trigger: Default for ordinary refinement targets where uncertainty can be reduced by the canonical ten-stage order.
technique_overlays.validation_expectation: Every non-first step consumes a named frame, artifact, decision, or ledger from an earlier step.

technique_overlays.trigger: The target names multiple plausible refinement routes, owners, or outputs and a bounded choice must be recorded before execution.
technique_overlays.validation_expectation: The dispatch includes a bounded route menu or records a blocked decision with the missing choice.

technique_overlays.trigger: The target contains competing principles, likely disagreements, or explore/exploit pressure.
technique_overlays.validation_expectation: Dialectic steps name roles, preserve stable disagreement, and leave unresolved residue in a ledger.

technique_overlays.trigger: Several candidate abstractions, designs, or implementation slices are plausible and premature convergence would be expensive.
technique_overlays.validation_expectation: Tournament use names roles, convergence criteria, rejected alternatives, Pareto criteria, and recomposition target.

technique_overlays.trigger: The target is hard to understand because its architecture, workflow, or relationships are hidden.
technique_overlays.validation_expectation: The route emits an explanation handle or requires an x-ray next route before implementation planning.

technique_overlays.trigger: A selected abstraction sounds plausible but needs a controlled failure test before planning or promotion.
technique_overlays.validation_expectation: The plan or repair step names expected toy-game evidence and how failure routes back to repair.

technique_overlays.trigger: Prior sessions, repository memory, or rejected candidates are part of the target context.
technique_overlays.validation_expectation: Recovered context is cited by handle, duplicate claims are not copied, and residue has an owner or next route.

technique_overlays.trigger: The target uses external research, sensitive context, or evidence that must not silently become canonical memory.
technique_overlays.validation_expectation: External or protected context is labelled, bounded, and prevented from becoming canonical without owner review.

subagent_strategy.trigger: The target may contain hidden structure, competing design pressures, or validation risk; Refine asks Dispatch Spec to make the role strategy explicit before execution.
subagent_strategy.explanation: Use role-bound sibling agents only when the selected overlays justify parallel critique, structure discovery, tournament comparison, memory recovery, or delegated verification. Refine remains the orchestrator and must show this strategy with context before asking permission to run.
context: Selected overlays drive the role set.
Subagents return receipts; they do not own lifecycle promotion.
Execution waits for operator approval after dispatch validation.

roles.purpose: Check whether selected overlays, gates, handoffs, and validation expectations match the problem shape.
Inspect hidden architecture, workflow, or evidence boundaries when x-ray, memory, protected-context, or toy-game overlays apply.

subagent_strategy.permission_prompt: Dispatch Spec recommends the listed subagent strategy for this refinement. Run these delegated reviewers before native runtime-backed stages?

steps.name: Context Builder evidence baseline
stop_conditions: block if strict context coverage cannot support a coherent seed

steps.name: Interrogation refine-review
convergence_criteria: stable disagreements are preserved as residue

steps.name: Distill
convergence_criteria: selected unit has a recomposition target

steps.name: Interrogation refine-design-review
convergence_criteria: repair candidates are separated from accepted design claims

gates.condition: REFINE-DISPATCH.json validates against dispatch.schema.yml and cited techniques are used by steps or gates.
If subagent_strategy.status is recommended or required, Refine shows the strategy, explains why with target context, and asks permission before delegated or native runtime-backed execution.
Each native runtime-backed stage declares a capability_ref and records a native stage receipt, approved subagent receipt, or explicit blocked reason before pass is claimed.
External research runs only when bounded research is selected or a named gap triggers confirmation.
Task Session, Sigil Development, Inventory, Ontology, Glossary, and Spellcraft authority is not claimed by Refine.
Every selected stage has an artifact path or blocked reason before RESULT.md claims completion.

native_stage_receipts.handoff_note: fill when Context Builder returns an artifact or blocked reason
fill when Invoke Define returns an artifact or blocked reason
fill when Interrogation returns a refine-review verdict or blocked reason
fill when Refine records the research decision
fill when Distill returns a coherent-unit artifact or blocked reason
fill when Invoke Design returns an artifact or blocked reason
fill when Interrogation returns a design-review verdict or blocked reason
fill when Distill Repair returns an artifact or blocked reason
fill when Invoke Plan returns an artifact or blocked reason
fill when final interrogation and Refine synthesis complete or block

promotion_guardrails: Refine may recommend Task Session or Sigil Development only after final synthesis.
Refine may not promote inventory, ontology, glossary, sigil, or spell artifacts.
Dispatch validation is route-shape evidence, not proof that semantic stage execution completed.
